frameworks.name: surround inhibition
frameworks.one_line: GABAergic lateral inhibition in V1 underlies size- and velocity-dependent perceptual suppression

frameworks.name: MEG gamma oscillations as inhibition index
frameworks.one_line: velocity-dependent gamma attenuation slope indexes inhibitory interactions in primary visual cortex

frameworks.name: spatial suppression psychophysics
frameworks.one_line: directional sensitivity drops for large high-contrast drifting gratings, more so at higher velocity

core_question: Does the slope of velocity-dependent MEG gamma attenuation in V1 predict individual differences in psychophysical spatial suppression?
key_assumptions: gamma response attenuation slope reflects cortical inhibitory strength
spatial suppression strength reflects surround inhibition
inter-individual variability is stable and measurable
manipulations: participant population (school-age boys vs adult women)
key_findings: GR attenuation slope predicted inter-individual SS in both samples
neuro-behavioral correlation was test-retest reliable across two sessions in adults
GR frequency and absolute amplitude did not correlate with SS
interpretations: spatial suppression is driven by inhibitory processes in human primary visual cortex
velocity-dependent gamma slope is a valid inhibitory marker linking neural and perceptual measures
